title: orchis basilica subsp. maculata
intro: Orchis basilica subsp. maculata, also known as the spotted green orchid, is a rare and unique subspecies of the orchid family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and is also known to have astringent properties that can help to stop bleeding. The health benefits of Orchis basilica subsp. maculata include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
